steps.m: A-L-M (special letters at the start of this chapter)
steps.n: These are special letters that appear at the start of some chapters. Each letter is said by its full name: alif (A), laam (L), meem (M). While scholars have discussed their meaning, Allah knows best what they mean.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The extra ي and pattern change makes it mean doing something gradually or repeatedly

steps.m: the sending down (of الْكِتٰبِ - the Book)
steps.n: The word gets a 'u' ending (ُ) because it's doing the action - it's the subject that's sending down the Book
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb form

This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. The word كِتَاب is in the genitive case because it comes after تَنزِيلُ in a possession relationship, where تَنزِيلُ (revelation) possesses/owns كِتَاب (book).
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by تَنزِيلُ (revelation). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form.
This is a basic negation particle that makes something not happen or not exist. In this ayah, it negates رَيْبَ (doubt), meaning 'there is no doubt'
Added fatha mark to show how the word is pronounced
Changes from verb 'to doubt' into the noun 'doubt'
Added fatha at the end because لا makes the word its object, showing what is being negated (there is no doubt).
This is a preposition that shows location or containment

steps.m: in it (referring to الْكِتٰبِ - the book)
steps.n: Added هِ which means 'it' and refers back to the book (الْكِتٰبِ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows what thing is being talked about.
This is a preposition that shows the book's revelation comes from the Lord of the worlds. It connects the two parts of the phrase together.
Doubling the ب adds emphasis to show complete mastery
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this connects it to العالمين (the worlds) that comes after it
Added shadda on first letter because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (تنزيل)
Forms noun meaning 'world/realm' from root meaning 'to know' - the world is what can be known through our senses. This word is possessed by رَبِّ (Lord) in the phrase 'Lord of the worlds'.
ين is added to make the word plural, showing there are many worlds or realms
Adding ال makes it specific - referring to all known worlds/realms. The َ at the end shows it's possessed by رَبِّ (Lord).
This is a question particle that introduces an alternative option. It's used here to ask if people will claim the Quran is made up, connecting to the previous discussion.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to invent or make up falsely

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Added Form VIII pattern showing he did this action for himself
Added ى to show the long 'aa' sound at the end of the word

steps.m: he made it up (object of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ meaning 'it' to show what was fabricated. This is what they are saying in the phrase 'they say he made it up'

steps.m: rather/but rather (contradicting what was claimed in أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ - 'Or do they say he made it up')
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a statement that corrects or contradicts what was said before. Here it's rejecting the claim that the Prophet made up the Quran.
Basic pronoun that refers back to something masculine mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers to الْحَقُّ (the truth) that comes right after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled letter with shadda makes it into a strong noun meaning the concept itself
Adding ال makes it specific. This word describes what هُوَ (it) is in this ayah.

steps.m: from (connects رَّبِّكَ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a helper word (preposition) that connects رَّبِّكَ (your Lord) to what comes before it. It shows where something comes from, like saying 'This message is from your Lord' in English.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
When مِن comes before this word, it adds emphasis by doubling the first letter (shown by the shadda)
The kasra (small diagonal line) at the end shows this means 'my Lord' - it shows possession/ownership
Adding كَ shows the lord belongs to the person being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The تُ prefix changes 'he warned' to 'you warn', and the middle vowel changes to match

steps.m: so that you warn (purpose for افْتَرَىٰهُ)
steps.n: The لِ shows this is explaining the purpose. This word explains why they claim he made it up (افْتَرَىٰهُ) in the previous ayah

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: The root meaning of standing together becomes a word for a group of people who stand as one community
Added tanween alif to show this refers to any group of people. This word is receiving the action of warning in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the word مَا which can act as a negative particle or question word depending on context.
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the negation. In this ayah, it negates the verb أَتَىٰهُم (came to them), meaning 'did not come to them'.
The alif is elongated (آ) for additional emphasis in pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning.
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) which is a small alif written above the ى. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added هُم at the end to show who received the action (them). This word appears in a verse saying no warner came to them before.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts, like saying 'from' in English
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word connects in pronunciation to the next word رَّبِّكَ (your Lord). This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to warn' into noun meaning 'warner'
Added shadda on ن and tanween kasra at end to show this is any warner (not a specific one) and is connected to the word مِن (from) in the ayah
This is a helper word that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from your house' or 'from the store' in English

steps.m: from (with emphasis because it's connected to نَّذِيرٍ)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger because it connects with the shadda on the word نَّذِيرٍ (warner) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to create a noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra to show this word follows مِن (from) in the ayah
Added كَ to show this refers to 'you' (Muhammad ﷺ). This whole phrase مِن قَبْلِكَ means 'from before you' in the context of previous messengers.
This is a word that shows hope or expectation for something to happen
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to a group of people (they/them). This word connects to يَهْتَدُونَ (they are guided) in the next ayah.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show someone finding guidance for themselves rather than guiding others
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
Basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what they describe. Here it connects Allah with what He did (created the heavens and earth).
Added ال with shadda to make it definite and specific. This shows we're talking about a specific being - Allah - who did something.

steps.m: to create from nothing, to make something new
he created (subject of the relative clause after ٱلَّذِى)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this is a completed action in the past. This word describes what Allah did, coming after ٱلَّذِى (the one who).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added hamza to create noun meaning the thing above us
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Added ال and shadda to make it definite
Added kasra at end because it's the object of the verb خَلَقَ (created) in this ayah
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the word 'earth'
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at the end to show this is what was created
Added و at beginning to connect it with السماوات (the heavens) that came before it
Added fatha to show this is the question word 'what'. In this ayah, it asks about what is between the heavens and earth.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, linking 'what is between them' to the previous mention of heavens and earth.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a preposition

steps.m: between them both (referring to السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added هُمَا which shows this refers to exactly two things (the heavens and earth mentioned before)
Basic preposition showing something happens inside a time or place. Here it shows that the creation happened within a specific time period (six days).
Adding the vowel mark changes it from a concept to a usable number word
Added the feminine marker ة to make it agree with أَيَّامٍ (days) which is feminine in Arabic
Added kasra to show this number belongs to or describes the word أَيَّامٍ (days) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Changes the word to its plural form by adding أ at the start and doubling the middle letter
Adds two dots under the word (tanween kasra) to show it is connected to the number 'six' before it
Shows this word is owned by/belongs to 'six' (سِتَّةِ)
This word connects two events in sequence - first Allah created the heavens and earth, then He rose over the throne. The shadda (ّ) on م makes the meaning stronger, showing these are distinct events.

steps.m: to be level, balanced, or complete

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show doing the action for oneself. In this ayah, it describes Allah establishing Himself on the throne.
The initial alif changes to hamzat al-wasl (ٱ) which helps with pronunciation when connecting words

Basic preposition showing position or authority. In this ayah, it connects the verb 'rose/established' (ٱسْتَوَىٰ) with 'the throne' (ٱلْعَرْشِ), showing that Allah established Himself over the throne.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun meaning 'throne'
Added ال to make it definite (the throne) and kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَى (upon/above) in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that refers to everything that exists between the heavens and earth. In this ayah, it's connecting to بَيْنَهُمَا (between them).
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being spoken to
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added shadda (double letter mark) to emphasize the meaning. This happens when it comes after certain letters like ل

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/less than'
Added kasra (ِ) because this word follows the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah
Added هِۦ to show this refers to Allah. The word is part of the phrase مِن دُونِهِۦ meaning 'besides Him' or 'other than Him'
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it comes from. Here it connects with دُونِهِۦ (besides Him) to show 'from besides Him'

steps.m: to protect, govern, or be close to

steps.n: Adding vowels changes the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to protect' or 'be close to'
Changed from a past tense verb (he protected) to a noun meaning protector/guardian
Added tanween kasra because it comes after مِن (from/of) in the ayah. The tanween also makes it indefinite (any protector rather than the protector).
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the negative particle
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word negates شَفِيعٍ (intercessor) in this ayah.

steps.m: to intercede, mediate, plead on behalf of others

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'one who intercedes a lot'
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (any intercessor) and is the object of the preposition مِن (from/of) in مِن شَفِيعٍ (of any intercessor)
This particle turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention to what follows
Adds فَ to show this question follows from what was just mentioned - in this case, about Allah creating everything without any other helper

steps.m: then will you not? (questioning why you don't تَتَذَكَّرُونَ - remember/take heed)
steps.n: Adds لا to make this a negative question, expressing surprise that people don't remember/take heed despite the clear signs just mentioned about Allah's power
Added a shadda (double letter) to change from 'remember' to 'make others remember/remind'
Added تَ at beginning to show doing action to oneself
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with extra care and attention
Changed to present tense to show ongoing management. This word connects to الْأَمْرَ (the affairs/matters) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Changed from action to noun meaning 'matter'. This is what's being arranged in the ayah.

steps.m: the matter (object of يُدَبِّرُ - He arranges)
steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it specific ('the matter')
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts from or comes out of
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven). This combination means 'from the sky/heaven'
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Adds ال to make it specific/definite, like changing 'a sky' to 'the sky'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah. Also elongated the alif with آ for emphasis.
This is a preposition that shows direction - in this ayah it connects the verb 'ascends' (يَعْرُجُ) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ), showing the direction of the action
Adding vowel marks to make the basic noun pattern
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it's connected to the word إِلَى (to) in the ayah
This word connects two actions in time order - first Allah manages affairs (يُدَبِّرُ), then the affairs ascend (يَعْرُجُ) back to Him. The shadda (ّ) makes the meaning stronger.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense base form
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix. The ُ (damma) on رُ and جُ show this is happening now. This word describes the ascension mentioned in the ayah from earth to heaven.
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added sukun (ْ) before attaching the pronoun. This is a helper step to prepare for connecting to the pronoun.

steps.m: to him (refers to يَعْرُجُ - it ascends)
steps.n: Added هِ (him) at the end. In this ayah, it refers back to what 'ascends' (يَعْرُجُ).
This is a preposition that shows something happens inside or within something else. Here it connects the verb يَعْرُجُ (ascends) with يَوْمٍ (a day) to show when the ascending happens.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: a day (in فِى يَوْمٍ - in a day)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is 'a day' and is being acted upon by the word فِى (in)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense

steps.m: he/it was (describing the length of يَوْمٍ/day)
steps.n: The middle letter و changed to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.

steps.m: to measure or set an amount

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added مِ at beginning and ا after second root letter to create a noun meaning 'amount/measure'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was)
Added هُ suffix to show possession (its)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The meaning shifted from joining things to the number 1000, since 1000 represents many hundreds joined together.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'was a thousand'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the base word
Adding feminine marker ة transforms the word into the noun meaning 'year'
Added two dots below (tanwin) to show this word is connected to 'thousand' - like saying 'a thousand years'
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together
The ن in مِن merges into the م of ما, shown by the shadda (ّ). This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic when these letters come together.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with a shadda
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' and changed vowels to ُ to show present tense
Added ونَ ending to show multiple people are doing the counting
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. It's a foundational word that doesn't come from a root.
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's elongated when pronounced.
Adding لِ changes the word from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away.
Adding كَ completes the word and emphasizes the distance of what's being pointed to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show who does the action, like changing 'know' to 'knower'
Changed the alif to a special long alif (ٰ) which is a writing variation

steps.m: the Knower (of الغيب والشهادة - the unseen and witnessed)
steps.n: Added U sound at the end to show this describes Allah as the main subject before الغيب والشهادة (the unseen and witnessed)

steps.m: to be absent or hidden from sight

steps.n: Changes from an action to a noun - from 'being hidden' to 'hidden thing/realm'. This word is possessed by عٰلِمُ (Knower), forming the phrase 'Knower of the hidden realm'.
Adding the definite article makes it specific
The ِ ending shows this word belongs to عٰلِمُ (Knower), making it mean 'of the hidden realm' in the phrase 'Knower of the hidden realm'.
Basic past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changed the verb into a noun that means the act of witnessing
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ش letter
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with the previous word

steps.m: and the witnessed (possessed by عٰلِمُ - Knower)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to عٰلِمُ (Knower), forming 'Knower of the witnessed'
Adding ي in the middle shows this quality is very strong - like saying someone is not just strong, but very strong

steps.m: The Mighty One (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Adding ال makes this refer specifically to Allah as The Mighty One

steps.m: The Mighty One (describing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows this is describing Allah directly in this ayah, connecting to الرَّحِيمُ (The Merciful) which comes after it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Forms intensive adjective pattern فَعِيل (fa'īl) meaning 'one who shows intense mercy'
Adding 'ال' (al-) makes it 'The' Merciful One - like a special title for Allah

This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things, like 'who' or 'which' in English. Here it connects to the verb أَحْسَنَ (perfected) to tell us about Allah who perfected everything.

steps.m: the one who/which (connects to أَحْسَنَ - perfected)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it specific. This changes it from just 'who/which' to 'the one who/which'.

steps.m: the one who/which (connects to أَحْسَنَ - perfected)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) at the end. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made excellent (the creation of كُلَّ شَىْءٍ)
steps.n: The verb changed from 'being good' to 'making something good' by adding أ at the start. Like changing from 'beautiful' to 'beautified'. Allah is the one doing the beautifying to everything He created.
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - changes from 'complete' to 'all/every'

steps.m: every, all (object of خَلَقَهُۥ - He created it)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb خَلَقَهُۥ (He created it) in this ayah
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object being perfected in the phrase 'perfected every thing'

steps.m: to create something from nothing, to make, to form

steps.n: Basic root letters got vowel marks (fatha) to make a past tense verb meaning 'he created'

steps.m: he created it (object of أَحْسَنَ - perfected)
steps.n: The هُۥ at the end shows what was created. This connects to the word أَحْسَنَ (perfected) earlier in the ayah, showing that Allah perfected everything He created.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows who did the action (he) and when it happened (in the past). This connects to the earlier verb أَحْسَنَ (he perfected) - both describing what Allah did.
The وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this action to what came before
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning the act of creation
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb بَدَأَ (he began)

steps.m: to be social or familiar with others

steps.n: Takes the root meaning 'to be social' and adds إِ and ان to make a noun meaning 'human' - humans are social beings
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) - this is just a spelling variation with no meaning change
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it (مِن طِينٍ - from clay)

steps.m: the human being (connected to مِن طِينٍ)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific - we're talking about a particular human being
This is a preposition showing that humans were created from clay. It connects the word خَلْقَ (creation) with طِينٍ (clay).
The kasra vowel mark changes this from a basic concept into a proper noun meaning clay
Added tanwin to show this is the material being used to create something (connected to مِن before it in the ayah)
Added damma (ُ) to show this is a complete word, not part of another word
Added shadda (ّ) to emphasize that this shows a clear sequence. In this ayah, it shows what Allah did next after a previous action - He made (جَعَلَ) his offspring.

steps.m: to make or set something in a particular way

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is connected to نَسْلَهُۥ (his offspring) which shows what he made.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb جَعَلَ (he made)
Adds 'his' (هُ) to show who the offspring belongs to. The word is still what is being made/created in the sentence.
Basic particle showing origin or source. In this ayah, it shows that offspring (نَسْلَهُ) comes from an extract (سُلٰلَةٍ).
The م gets a shadda (doubled) because it comes before another م in مَّآءٍ. This is just for easier pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to draw out or extract something from something else

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to extract' into a noun meaning 'something extracted' by adding vowels and stretching the middle
Added ة at the end to show we're talking about one specific instance of something being extracted
Added ٍ at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from)
Basic form of the word that shows something comes from or is made of something else. In this ayah, it shows that نَسْلَهُ (his offspring) comes from سُلٰلَةٍ (an extract).
The م gets doubled (shown by ّ) because it's followed by another م in مَّهِينٍ. This is just for easier pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
The root letters م-و-ه transform to make the common word for 'water' (ماء) by changing و to ا and adding ء at the end
When مِن comes before this word, the م gets a shadda (double letter) to make it stronger
The long alif makes the word stronger and more emphatic
The word gets a double kasra (ٍ) at the end and a shadda (ّ) at the start to show it means 'of water' after مِن
Added ي and vowels to make it mean 'very weak/worthless' - like turning 'weak' into 'weakling'
Adds two dots under the last letter to show it connects to the word 'water'
The doubled م makes the meaning stronger, like saying 'very' or 'completely'

steps.m: then (connects to سَوَّىٰهُ - He fashioned him)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows sequence of events. The damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) make it a complete word. In this ayah, it connects to what happens next - that Allah fashioned the human.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show extra effort or thoroughness in the action
Added هُ at the end to show who received the action. This word comes after ثُمَّ (then) in the ayah.
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This describes Allah breathing life into His creation.

steps.m: and He blew (connected to سَوَّىٰهُ - He fashioned him)
steps.n: The وَ shows this action happened after Allah fashioned/shaped the human form (سَوَّىٰهُ), connecting these two steps of creation
This shows where something happens

steps.m: in him/it (referring to the soul/spirit being breathed)
steps.n: Added هِ to show who or what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the soul/spirit being breathed. This word connects with نَفَخَ (he breathed) to show what was breathed into.

steps.m: from (connects رُّوحِهِ to the previous phrase)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows the spirit (روح) is coming from Him. It connects the word 'spirit' (روح) to the previous phrase about Allah breathing.
Root letters ر-و-ح got a damma (u sound) to make the word for 'spirit'
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added kasra to show this word is owned by or connected to something else
Added هِۦ to show it belongs to Him (Allah). This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set
he made/set (connected to سَوَّىٰهُ - he fashioned him)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This continues the sequence of actions after Allah fashioned humans.

steps.m: and he made (connected to سَوَّىٰهُ - he fashioned him)
steps.n: The وَ shows this is part of a sequence: first Allah fashioned humans, then breathed His spirit into them, and then made for them hearing, sight, and hearts.
Basic particle showing direction or purpose

steps.m: for you all (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word connects to جَعَلَ (He made) to show who receives the action.
Changed from action (hear) to the sense of hearing itself. This is what Allah made for people, as shown by the verb جَعَلَ (he made/created).

steps.m: the hearing (object of جَعَلَ - he made)
steps.n: Added ٱل at the beginning to mean 'the'

steps.m: the strong hearing (object of جَعَلَ - he made)
steps.n: Added a doubled letter (ّ) to show this is THE hearing sense, making it definite and specific

steps.m: the strong hearing (object of جَعَلَ - he made)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end because this word receives the action of جَعَلَ (he made)

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic noun for one instance of sight
Changed the word to show many sights instead of just one

steps.m: the sights (connected to السَّمْعَ - the hearing)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show it's connected to 'hearing' in the verse

steps.m: and the sights (connected to السَّمْعَ - the hearing)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with 'hearing' that came before it
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for 'heart'
Changes from single heart (فؤاد) to plural hearts (أفئدة) - a special plural form
Adds 'the' to make it specific
Adds 'and' to connect it with previous words (hearing and sight) in the verse
Changes from verb 'to be small' into adjective meaning 'small/few in amount'

steps.m: little (describes how much you give thanks)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word describes how much the action happens. It connects to تَشْكُرُونَ (you give thanks) to say 'you give thanks little'
Basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَشْكُرُونَ (you give thanks), showing what little thanks is given.
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the word, making the 'm' sound stronger. This emphasizes how little thanks is given.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he thanked'
Changed vowels to show ongoing action instead of past action
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show many people are doing the action together
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Adding وا at the end makes it refer to a group of people (they) instead of just one person (he)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is the basic word meaning 'when' that refers to a future time

steps.m: is it when? (questioning what comes after ضَلَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added the questioning prefix أَءِ to express disbelief or surprise about something. In this ayah, it's questioning the possibility of being recreated after being lost in the earth.
Added fathas to make basic past tense verb
Changed final fatha to sukun to prepare for adding 'we'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a primitive preposition that cannot be derived from a root. It is one of the basic building blocks of Arabic grammar.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows فِى (in) in the ayah
Added نا at the end to show this refers to 'we'

steps.m: is it that we? (questioning what comes in لَفِى خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ)
steps.n: Added أَ (a) at the start to turn this into a question meaning 'is it that...?'. The second hamza (ء) helps separate the two a-sounds.
Basic preposition meaning 'in' - this is the starting form since it's a particle without a root
Added emphatic لَ at beginning to emphasize certainty. This word is part of the phrase أَءِنَّا لَفِى meaning 'are we surely in...'

steps.m: to create or form something new

steps.n: Changed from action to noun showing the process itself
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite and being acted upon in the sentence
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective that describes how something is new. This word describes خَلْقٍ (creation) in this ayah.
Added the ٍ mark at the end to show this word follows خَلْقٍ (creation) in the sentence, like saying 'a new creation'

steps.m: rather/but rather (contrasts with previous statement أَءِذَا ضَلَلْنَا)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows contrast with what came before. Here it introduces a different reason why they won't be in a new creation - because they disbelieve in meeting their Lord.
Basic form of the third person masculine plural pronoun
Added damma (ُ) to show this is acting as a subject. In this ayah, هُم is the subject of كٰفِرُونَ (they are disbelievers).
Adding vowel marks changes this into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Changes the verb into a noun that describes the action itself
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by or connected to the word that follows it (their Lord)

steps.m: in/with the meeting (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is about being in or with something
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle letter ب with shadda shows this is an intensive form, meaning one who completely and perfectly fulfills this role

steps.m: Lord (possessed by or connected to something)
steps.n: Adding kasra shows this word is connected to something after it

steps.m: their Lord (referring to those who deny the meeting with Him)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows possession by them. In this ayah, 'them' refers to those who deny they will meet Allah after being recreated.

steps.m: to reject, deny, cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed pattern to show someone who does this action. This describes who 'they' (هم) are in the verse.
Added damma to show this is the subject
Changed to plural form by adding ون, and alif became elongated (ٰ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to tell the people about death)
steps.n: Changed to a command form. The middle letter و drops out because it's between two consonants in a command. This word starts a statement about death that Allah commands the Prophet ﷺ to tell the people.
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he' form
Doubling the middle letter makes the action more thorough or complete
Adding ت makes it reflexive - the action comes back to the doer
Changes to present/future tense. In this ayah, this refers to the Angel of Death taking souls.
Adds كُم to show who is being taken (you all), and extends the alif (ىٰ) for pronunciation
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowels 'a' to mean 'angel' - a special messenger of God
The shadda emphasizes this is specifically the Angel of Death mentioned in this ayah who is appointed over taking souls
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject doing the action in this part of the ayah
Adding the vowel marks changes it from a root to a proper noun meaning 'death'
Added ال (the) at the start and ِ (kasra) at the end to show it's 'the death' and connects to the word angel
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of sentences, like saying 'The person who came' or 'The book that I read'
Added ال with shadda at the beginning to make it more specific. In this ayah, it refers to the Angel of Death mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense pattern

steps.m: he was put in charge (by مَّلَكُ ٱلْمَوْتِ - the angel of death)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show the angel of death was given this responsibility
Added kasra (ِ) to show how the letter is pronounced

steps.m: by/with you all (object of وُكِّلَ - was put in charge)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to وُكِّلَ (was put in charge) in the ayah, showing who was put in charge over whom.
This is the basic form of the word that connects events in sequence
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) which are essential parts of this word's structure. This word connects the action of taking souls (يَتَوَفَّىٰكُم) to what happens after.
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to رَبِّكُمْ (your Lord) to show where you are being returned to.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely - used especially for Allah as the ultimate caretaker
The kasra shows this word is connected to what comes after it (in this case, showing whose Lord)

steps.m: your Lord (referring to all those being addressed)

steps.m: you (all) are returned (to رَبِّكُمْ - your Lord)

Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Connected root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Added تَ prefix to change subject to 'you' and changed final vowel to damma for present tense
The weak letter ي changes to ى at the end of the word
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) as pronunciation helpers

The sukun (ْ) changes to kasra (ِ) because it connects to the word after it (المجرمون). This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action
Added ال to make it specific
Added ون to show it's talking about multiple criminals
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter and kasra before last root letter to show who does the action. This describes المجرمون (the criminals) who are doing the lowering.
Added plural ending وا to show multiple people doing the action. This matches with المجرمون (the criminals) who are the subject.
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'head'
Changes to broken plural form to show multiple heads
Adds kasra at end to show the word is the object of a preposition

steps.m: their heads (the heads of the criminals mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Adds pronoun 'their' to show who the heads belong to. Here it refers back to 'the criminals' (ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a common preposition in Arabic that comes from the idea of being present with something

steps.m: near/in the presence of (رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: The root letters ع-ن-د become عِنْدَ (near/in presence of) to show location. In this ayah, it tells us where - near their Lord.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop, sustain and bring to completion

steps.m: their Lord (the one who nurtures and sustains them)

Adding نا shows possession - 'our Lord'

Adding أ at the beginning makes the seeing more intense or clear, rather than causative in this context
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. In this ayah, this is part of what the sinners say when they finally see the truth clearly.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to hear'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.n: Adds 'so/then' to connect this command to what came before it

Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: we do/work (requesting to do righteous deeds)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and changed middle vowel to kasra to show who does the action

steps.m: to be completely sure of something

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha and kasra) to make it mean 'he became certain' in the past

Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. When لو comes after و, it often means 'even if' rather than just 'if'.

This root carries the basic meaning of bringing or coming

steps.m: every, all (object of لَءَاتَيْنَا - we would have given)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

The ين ending makes it refer to many people or things together

steps.m: your day (referring to the meeting/encounter mentioned in لِقَآءَ)

Adding نَ at the end makes it refer to a group of females who did the action
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) when connecting to suffixes
Added كُمْ to show who was forgotten/abandoned

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.n: Added هُ to show what was made/set - refers back to the Book (الكتاب) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added damma and sukun to create a noun meaning the concept of guidance
The ي changes to ى and tanwin (double marks) is added at the end to show this is a special kind of guidance from Allah
Adding vowel marks to create basic past tense verb form
Added لِ (li) at the start to mean 'for/to'. Also, بَنِي here means 'children/descendants', not 'he built'
Added shadda on ل to emphasize the meaning
Changed spelling of final ي to ى with a small mark above to show it belongs to/connects with إِسْرٰٓءِيلَ (Israel)

This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew meaning 'one who struggles with God'. It refers to Prophet Jacob (Ya'qub) who was given this name. In this ayah, 'children of Israel' refers to the descendants of Prophet Jacob.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did this action. This word is doing the action of making أَئِمَّةً (leaders).
Added وَ at the start to mean 'and'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic letters rather than a root.

steps.m: from them (referring to those who were made leaders in وَجَعَلْنَا مِنْهُمْ أَئِمَّةً)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show it means 'them' (masculine plural). This connects back to the people who Allah made into leaders (أَئِمَّةً) as mentioned later in the verse.

steps.m: to lead or be in front

steps.n: Basic past tense verb form - added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make 'he led'
Changes verb 'he led' into noun meaning 'leader' by adding letters at start and middle
Changes single leader to multiple leaders with special plural form

steps.m: leaders (as the object being made by Allah)
steps.n: The tanween at the end shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلْنَا (We made) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with sukun and kasra on middle letter. The final ى changes back to ي.

steps.m: they guide (leaders who guide with Allah's command)
steps.n: Changed to plural by adding ون. The ي at the end is dropped. This word refers to the leaders (أَئِمَّةً) mentioned earlier who guide by Allah's command (بِأَمْرِنَا).
Changed from verb 'to command' into the noun 'a command' by adding vowel marks
Added بِ at beginning to show how or by what means something happens
Added kasra at end to show this word is possessed by something
Added نَا at end to show the command belongs to us
Time particle that means 'when' or 'after'. The shadda indicates emphasis.
These are completely different words - 'lam' means 'not' while 'lamma' means 'to gather'. The shadda here creates a new word, not a modification of the first word.
Adding the vowels and ا at the end makes it specifically mean 'when' or 'after' - connecting the first action (We made them leaders) with the second action (they were patient)
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This word describes the leaders (أَئِمَّةً) who guide (يَهْدُونَ) in this verse.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state/condition

steps.n: The root letters ك-و-ن got vowel marks to make it past tense (he was). The middle letter و turned into ا, which is a normal change for this type of word.

steps.m: they were/existed (referring to the أَئِمَّةً 'leaders' mentioned before)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that many people (the leaders) did this action together

steps.m: and they were/existed (connecting to how they guide by Our command)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase about how these leaders guide by Allah's command
Root letters combine with long 'aa' sound and 'ah' ending to make a noun meaning 'sign'
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
Adds 'our' to show these are Allah's signs

steps.m: in/by our clear signs (that they firmly believe)
steps.n: Adds بِ at the beginning to show these are the signs they believe in, connecting to يُوقِنُونَ (they firmly believe)

steps.m: to be completely certain beyond any doubt

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he became certain'
Changed from past tense to present tense by adding ي and و, showing someone who is certain now
Added plural ending to show multiple people having certainty. This word describes what the people were doing in relation to وَكَانُوا (and they were)
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what follows it, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English. Here it emphasizes that your Lord will definitely judge between them.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: The root letters ر-ب-ب became the word 'Lord' (رَبَّ) with a fatha ending because it follows إِنَّ (indeed)

steps.m: your Lord (being acted upon by إِنَّ indeed/truly)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is your Lord. The word is part of the phrase إِنَّ رَبَّكَ (indeed, your Lord)
This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is the subject (doer) of the verb يَفْصِلُ (judges/decides between).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense. This word relates to هُوَ (He) which refers to رَبَّكَ (your Lord) who will judge between them.
Adds ُ to show this is the main verb doing the action in the sentence
Forms a preposition meaning 'between'. This word shows what will be judged in the phrase 'he judges between them'.

steps.m: between them (object of يَفْصِلُ - he judges)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ at the end to show who is being judged between. The whole phrase means 'he judges between them'.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و takes a sukun (ْ) because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: day (object of يَفْصِلُ - He will judge)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is telling us when the judging will happen. It's connected to the verb يَفْصِلُ (He will judge) in the ayah.
Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'those who stand together'
The word changes from meaning 'people' to meaning 'the act of standing up/rising' by changing the middle letter and vowels. This shows it's now about the action.
Adding ة makes this refer to the specific event of resurrection rather than just any rising
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific, known resurrection
The ِ ending shows this word belongs to يَوْمَ (day), forming 'Day of Resurrection'. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ).
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'

steps.m: in what (they used to differ about)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what' to specify what something is in. This word connects to يَخْتَلِفُونَ (they differ) in the ayah, showing what they differ about.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special 'weak' letter in the middle of a past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did this action. This word is the subject of يَخْتَلِفُونَ (they differ) in this ayah.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'in'

steps.m: in it/him (referring to what they differed about)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show what is being referred to. This connects to يَخْتَلِفُونَ (they differ), showing what they differ about.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added letters to show people disagreeing back and forth with each other
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word relates to فِيهِ (in it) in the ayah, showing what they disagree about.
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding 'is it that?' at the start of a sentence
The و adds 'and' to connect this question with previous thoughts

steps.m: and have they not (before يَهْدِ - guide)
steps.n: The لَمْ makes the following verb negative and past tense. Here it negates يَهْدِ (guide), asking 'has it not guided them?'

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb 'he guided' by adding the vowel marks fatHa
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Final ي is dropped because the word comes after أَوَلَمْ (and did not...), which makes it conditional
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ to show who the action is for - in this case, it refers to the people being guided in يَهْدِ (guides)

steps.m: how many (referring to أَهْلَكْنَا - we destroyed)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about quantity. In this ayah, it's asking about how many previous generations were destroyed, showing astonishment at the number.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to make the action affect others - changes from 'perish' to 'make perish/destroy'
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'
Basic preposition showing starting point or origin. In this ayah, it connects the word قَبْلِ (before) with هِم (them) to mean 'before them'

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the word mean 'before' as a direction or time word
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِم at the end to show this means 'before them', referring to the previous nations mentioned in the verse
This is the basic form of the preposition meaning 'from'
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show importance or certainty
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this connects to the next word الْقُرُونِ (the generations)

steps.m: to join together in time (like generations following each other)
a generation (group of people living at the same time)
steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowels - makes it mean 'one generation of people'
Changed vowels and added و to make it plural, showing many generations

steps.m: the generations (that were destroyed before them)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to show these are specific generations mentioned in the ayah, and ِ to show this word follows مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb. The final ي changes to ى when it comes at the end.
Changes from 'he walked' (past) to 'he walks' (present) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Changed from 'he walks' to 'they walk' by adding ون at the end
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows that the walking happens in their dwellings/homes (مَسٰكِنِهِمْ).

steps.m: to live somewhere, to settle down

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) to make past tense 'he' form of verb meaning 'to dwell'
Adds م prefix to show this is a place where dwelling happens
Changes to broken plural form by adding long a sound after first letter and i sound before last letter
Added هِمْ at the end to show these are their dwellings

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes فِى ذٰلِكَ لَءَايٰتٍ that follows)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that there are definitely signs in what was mentioned before.
This is a basic preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it shows that they walk in/within their dwellings.
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's a demonstrative.
Adding لِ makes it point to something farther away instead of nearby

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to the signs/evidence mentioned in the previous words)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word and makes it specifically point to something far away. In this ayah, it's pointing to the evidence mentioned about previous destroyed nations.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
The hamza (ء) was written separately at the start - same word and meaning
Changed to plural form by replacing ة with ات
Adds 'for/to' meaning to the word
The word gets a tanwin (double dots) at the end to show it means 'signs' in general, not specific signs. Works with 'indeed in that' before it.
Basic negative particle
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before

steps.m: then will they not? (subject of يَسْمَعُونَ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it a rhetorical question. This word connects to يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear) in the next ayah, asking 'Will they not hear?'

steps.m: to hear or listen to sounds

steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show someone is doing this action now, and added vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: they hear/listen (referring to the people who walk in their dwellings)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to the people mentioned earlier who walk in the dwellings of destroyed nations.
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added و which connects this question to what came before it
Added لَمْ which makes the verb negative in the past tense. This particle connects to the verb يَرَوْا (they see) that comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to drive or lead something forward
steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: we drive/lead (the water to the barren land)
steps.n: Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, changed middle vowels to show present tense, and added ُ at end to show it's happening now

steps.m: to flow or spread like water

steps.n: The root letters م-و-ه form the word for water (ماء) by changing و to ا and adding ء at the end. This is a common way Arabic makes nouns from root letters.

steps.m: the water (object of نَسُوقُ - we drive)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this water is what Allah is driving to the barren land. The alif is written long (آ) because it comes before hamza.
This is a preposition that shows where Allah drives the water - to the barren land. It connects the verb نَسُوقُ (We drive) with its destination ٱلْأَرْضِ (the land).
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The kasra ending shows this word follows the preposition إِلَى (to) in the ayah
Added damma vowels to make it a noun describing barren land that can't grow plants
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon by نَسُوقُ (we drive) in the verse

steps.m: to exit or make something exit

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'causing something to exit' instead of just 'exiting'
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: and so we bring out (crops with الْمَآءَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows from the previous one (sending water). The object is crops (زَرْعًا) which comes in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: with/by means of (connected to فَنُخْرِجُ - then We bring forth)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by means of'. This shows how something is done.

steps.m: with it/by means of it (referring to ٱلْمَآءَ - the water)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه (him/it) to show what we're doing something with. Here it refers back to 'the water' mentioned earlier.

steps.m: with it/by means of it (referring to ٱلْمَآءَ - the water)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide to show the هِ should be clearly pronounced.

steps.m: crops (object of نُخْرِجُ - we bring forth)
steps.n: Changed from action to the thing itself (crops). This word is what's being brought forth in the verse.
Added ًا to show this is what's being acted upon - the crops that are being brought forth.

Basic preposition indicating source or origin

Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb about comfort into a plural word meaning animals that help humans (like cows, camels, sheep)
Changed the alif to an alif with overbar (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added possession suffix to show whose cattle. This word is the subject of تَأْكُلُ (eats/grazes) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a word meaning one self/soul
This is a broken plural form that changes the internal structure of the word to show multiple souls/selves
Added هُمْ to show these souls belong to them
Added conjunction وَ meaning 'and'. This word is coordinated with أَنْعٰمُهُمْ (their cattle) as subjects of تَأْكُلُ (eat)
Starting with the question particle أَ which turns a statement into a question
Added فَ to show this question follows from what was mentioned before

steps.m: then do they not? (connected to يُبْصِرُونَ - see)
steps.n: Added لا to make this a negative question. This word connects to يُبْصِرُونَ (see) in the next ayah, asking 'then do they not see?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are seeing
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a special letter.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, and changed the vowels to show ongoing action
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
This is a basic question word that asks about time. It is not derived from a root.
Added short 'a' vowel marks (fatha) to show how to pronounce this question word that asks 'when?'

steps.m: when? (asking about the time of الْفَتْحُ/the victory)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) to show elongation of the final sound. This word is asking about when the victory mentioned in the ayah will come.
This is the basic pointing word that shows something specific. Like pointing with your finger and saying 'that'.
Added ه at the beginning to get attention, like saying 'hey, look at this!'
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ه and the fatha mark (َ) on ذ to help with pronunciation. This word is what they are saying in يَقُولُونَ (they say).
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he opened' (action) to 'opening/victory' (thing) by making middle letter silent
Added the definite article ال (written as ٱلْ) to make it specific
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say)
This is a basic word that introduces a condition. It connects 'if' with what comes after it to show what depends on what. Here it introduces the condition 'if you are truthful'.
Added the kasra mark (ِ) under the إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'in'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Command form - shortened by dropping middle و and changing vowels to tell someone 'Be!'
Added تُمْ to show past tense for multiple people being addressed
Word is already complete - no additional changes required
Adds vowels to show someone being truthful at a specific time
Adding alif makes it into the pattern of someone who does the action regularly
Adding ين makes it plural for masculine nouns
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و because it's between two consonants. This tells one person to do something.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word tells us when something happens. It's connected to الفَتْحِ (the victory) to mean 'on the day of victory'

steps.m: to open or make a way through something
victory (the act of opening the way to success)
steps.n: Changed from action to the result of that action. Like how 'to win' becomes 'victory'.

steps.m: the victory (referring to a specific victory)
steps.n: Added 'the' to show we're talking about a specific victory mentioned in this context

steps.m: of the victory (in يَوْمَ ٱلْفَتْحِ - day of the victory)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word is connected to يَوْمَ (day) before it, making the phrase 'day of the victory'

Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'

This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about multiple people

Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show this belongs to them. This word is the subject of يَنفَعُ (benefits) in this ayah.
This is a basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'. In this ayah, it's used to negate يَنفَعُ (benefit), showing that the faith won't benefit those who disbelieved.

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِنْتَ to show waiting while watching/expecting something - like actively waiting
Changed from 'he waited' to 'wait!' by changing the vowels and dropping the last vowel to make it a command

Added اِنْتَ at the beginning to show doing the action with purpose and expectation
